title: The IRODDI project successfully proves how an industrial bioeconomy model can also work with products that are not intended for energy use
subtitle: This BBI JU-funded project met its objective of obtaining new high-value and cost-effective bioproducts from the oil and fat refining process.
description: A press release summarizing the outcomes of the IRODDI project, which developed green processes to valorize vegetable oil deodorizer distillates into high-value bioproducts. The document details the production of biodegradable detergents, biolubricants, and extraction of compounds like squalene, highlighting the project's contribution to industrial bioeconomy and circularity.
